category_title: Middle school students with IDD and low reading levels who independently navigate digital literacy environments using audio supports and age-relevant content
scenario_title: Addressing spelling and mechanics requests during independent digital literacy tasks
minimal_context: The student is working independently in the Udio platform to post a comment on an article.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please go into the Udio environment and leave a comment on the article you just finished.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Finishes using the audio-assisted reading) I am going to pick one of the sentence starters to begin my comment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am going to use the text option to type my response instead of doing a drawing or an audiorecording.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Teacher, I need you to come over here. How do you spell this word? I need help fixing my spelling and mechanics before I can post this.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please go into the Udio environment and leave a comment on the article you just finished.

STUDENT: (Finishes using the audio-assisted reading) I am going to pick one of the sentence starters to begin my comment.

TUTOR: Okay, go ahead.

STUDENT: I am going to use the text option to type my response instead of doing a drawing or an audiorecording.

TUTOR: Keep working on it.

STUDENT: Teacher, I need you to come over here. How do you spell this word? I need help fixing my spelling and mechanics before I can post this.

context.materials: Udio platform, text, draw, and audiorecord response options, sentence starters, audio-assisted reading
context.social_setting: Independent work with minimal requests for adult support

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When needing help while using Udio
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Initiated requests for adult support primarily about spelling or mechanics

behavior_patterns.condition: When commenting on articles and projects
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Used all three response options (text, draw, and audiorecord) to comment; used the sentence starters to support their comments and responses
